flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating that the registry-facing receiver was not ready, not admissible, or could not be treated as ready at the delivery boundary.
supporting_entity_names: Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Registry / Delivery Port;Log Level Records Registry
trigger_context: Dispatcher checks readiness/admission through Registry / Delivery Port before or during a delivery attempt.
post_delivery_effect: Delivery does not complete; queue-container may need to remain pending, be retried, or be requalified depending on runtime state.
anti_collapse_rule: Readiness / Not-Ready Flag must remain compact state and must not become a detailed Delivery Attempt Report or Backpressure Report.
notes: This flag records state only; diagnostic explanation belongs to dispatcher feedback reports.

flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating that the dispatcher-registry handoff completed successfully.
supporting_entity_names: Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Registry / Delivery Port;Log Level Records Registry
trigger_context: Registry handoff is accepted and delivery is considered complete at the assembler boundary.
post_delivery_effect: Envelope lifecycle authority transfers to Log Level Records Registry; assembler-side local runtime state may be released or requalified.
anti_collapse_rule: Delivery Success Flag must not imply that Assembler retains Log Level Envelope lifecycle authority after valid handoff.
notes: Success is a delivery-cycle flag; lifecycle transfer is governed separately by GOV-003 and BCO-012 handoff semantics.

flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating that a delivery attempt failed or could not be completed after entering delivery handling.
supporting_entity_names: Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Single Queue Moderator;Registry / Delivery Port
trigger_context: A delivery attempt encounters failure before valid lifecycle transfer to the registry.
post_delivery_effect: Local runtime state may require reset, requalification, or re-entry into delivery waiting discipline.
anti_collapse_rule: Delivery Failure Flag must not expand into Failure Diagnostic Report; it only records compact failure state.
notes: Detailed failure context belongs to RSP-CAN-048.2 / Dispatcher Feedback Report.

flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating receiving-side refusal, non-admission, or explicit rejection at the registry-facing boundary.
supporting_entity_names: Registry / Delivery Port;Log Level Records Registry;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager
trigger_context: Registry / Delivery Port or receiving side indicates refusal or non-admission.
post_delivery_effect: Delivery does not complete; local recovery or requalification may be required depending on slot and queue-container state.
anti_collapse_rule: Refusal Flag must not collapse into Refusal Diagnostic Report.
notes: The flag records that refusal occurred; detailed refusal context belongs to RSP-CAN-048.3.

flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating capacity, pressure, or throttling condition preventing delivery progression.
supporting_entity_names: Registry / Delivery Port;Log Level Records Registry;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager
trigger_context: Readiness/capacity signal indicates backpressure or effective capacity blocking.
post_delivery_effect: Delivery is deferred, degraded, or requeued according to delivery round and queue-container requalification rules.
anti_collapse_rule: Backpressure Flag must not collapse into Backpressure Report.
notes: The flag is compact state; detailed backpressure context belongs to RSP-CAN-048.4.

flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating assembler-local runtime cleanup, slot release, or state restoration is required after delivery outcome.
supporting_entity_names: Single Queue Moderator;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager
trigger_context: Delivery outcome requires local reset of slot lifecycle, queue-container readiness, or delivery-side waiting discipline.
post_delivery_effect: Single Queue Moderator and Delivery Round Manager must restore local runtime consistency without claiming registry lifecycle ownership.
anti_collapse_rule: Local Reset Required Flag must not imply registry-side lifecycle ownership or envelope lifecycle control.
notes: Flag points to local assembler runtime restoration only.

flag_semantics: Compact delivery-cycle state indicating the affected Queue Container Identifier must be requalified before returning to writing or delivery waiting discipline.
supporting_entity_names: Single Queue Moderator;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Writing / Feeding Round Manager
trigger_context: Delivery outcome changes queue-container eligibility or slot lifecycle state enough to require requalification.
post_delivery_effect: Queue Container Identifier returns to the correct waiting list or runtime progression path after moderation.
anti_collapse_rule: Queue Requalification Required Flag must not cause waiting lists to store Log Level Envelopes; waiting lists store Queue Container Identifiers.
notes: This flag preserves queue-container locator discipline after delivery-cycle outcomes.
